title: Why is there only one set of Dead Sea Scrolls
display_title: Why is there only one set of Dead Sea Scrolls
wikitext:
[]: Why is there only one set of Dead Sea Scrolls?             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:09 pm The Dead Sea Scrolls, so far as I understand them, where found in Antarctica and prophesized the arrival and order of the Angels, as well as serving as a manual for the Seed. My question is, why was there only one? If I'm assuming correctly that the Scrolls came with Adam, why didn't Lilith come with one? And why would they include the arrival of the Angels if there would be no one else to read the scrolls? An argument that came to my mind was that, like Lilith's Lance, it was destroyed, but I don't recall ever hearing about any Scrolls associated with her. And if an argument for the Scrolls giving the order of the Angels is for the Angels themselves to read later, well, how does that make sense? I don't see them as beings that can or would read such things, especially since they're isolated to themselves as individuals, and can't have connections with each other (without, say, causing an Impact).

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:14 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/Dead_Sea_Scrolls If Lilith did come with a set of scrolls just as Adam did (and I think it would make sense for both to have a lance and set of scrolls), then it was probably destroyed along with the lance during 1I.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:43 pm I've read that, but I still don't see why it would list the order of the Angels' awakenings. EDIT: Would Lilith's DSS list the order of all Lilim that were to come into existence?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Oct 25, 2012 9:49 pm it's....better not to think to deeply about DSS as they're just about the most bizarre and mysterious part of the whole series mythology.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Thu Oct 25, 2012 10:17 pm Merkaba wrote: EDIT: Would Lilith's DSS list the order of all Lilim that were to come into existence?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 10:36 pm Lurkis, your response is exactly what I was looking for and had never thought of. Although... I saw another topic that asked whether or not there were two kinds of seeds. Your answer, I assume, operates under the assumption that there are only two kinds. However, I'm wondering why, if there are only two types, would there be an odd number (seven, to be precise) sent throughout the universe? Furthermore, if post-Instrumentality Lilim can reclaim their previous shapes, and the FAR, fearing the end of their world, loaded up their own souls into these seeds and dispersed themselves though out space, why the FAR themselves would pick either to live isolated lives as Angels or depression-filled lives as Lilin, without the ability to return to their original states of consciousness. (I'm connecting this, albeit loosely, to why the FAR would need DSS to remind themselves of the other FAR whose souls would manifest as Angels - why they couldn't remember what came before as the post-Instrumentality Lilim, who again are FAR themselves, can.)

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Thu Oct 25, 2012 10:49 pm Your idea of having more than just the two types of seeds does intrigue me, something in the back of my mind tells me it's been answered before I just can't think of where. I don't think the FAR are capable of seeing or existing through us or the angels. More of a "We are dying, we have come to terms with this so let's do one last thing before we die. Send out our souls and create life throughout the galaxy." In their eyes, it could have been seen as living on, much how SEELE want's to live on forever in an arc. So having the DSS was for us and the other species as a second safeguard next to the Lance. Note* I thought it was only one FAR soul per seed? O.o if this is wrong please correct me.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Thu Oct 25, 2012 11:01 pm I think, when reading Reichu's analysis of the video game that presents the backtory for the FAR, they were a "race of beings." She also said that there were seven seeds sent out, and the topic I brought up started, and remained in, speculation as to whether there were only the two types of seeds among the seven. I assume that the "race of beings" had more than seven beings, so I think there would be more than one in each seed. But yeah, following that logic, plus the part about how returning to LCL doesn't erase the memory of one's soul, I'm wondering why the FAR, who are the souls that filled the Chamber of Guf (from which all conscious life builds their physical selves around/from) and subsequently all of the Lilin and Angels in the Evaverse (main cast included), can't remember themselves as FAR. And, oh no, as I was typing that, the theory of Mari being a FAR popped into my head, and it made sense. *gulp* Since every Lilin and Angel are the re-embodiments of the FAR, maybe she somehow retained those memories, and thus she knows how to operate the Evas in ways others don't, being that the FAR were essentially gods of technology. PS - Thanks for going down this crazy path of speculation with me.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:11 pm But yeah, following that logic, plus the part about how returning to LCL doesn't erase the memory of one's soul, I'm wondering why the FAR, who are the souls that filled the Chamber of Guf (from which all conscious life builds their physical selves around/from) and subsequently all of the Lilin and Angels in the Evaverse (main cast included), can't remember themselves as FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:20 pm Per the CI there were seven Seeds total, so make of that what you will. As for the SDSS, they're an operating manual. They don't predict the Angels' appearances in terms of dates and such; instead they say "this is how Seed X works. Once activated it will do A, B, and C, and the offspring produced will mature like so." So they look at what happened in 2I and do some math and bam, prophecies. They make it sound all religious and shit because they're religious nutjobs, but it could just as easily be a technical manual. The real question is a) how the fuck did they survive for three and a half billion years, and b) how on Earth did anyone manage to translate the things? Translating a language without a Rosetta Stone of some sort is nigh-impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:26 pm Lurkis wrote: That is why I think only Seeds of Life contain the DSS, for SoK to understand and prepare.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:45 pm Reichu wrote: Both Adam and Lilith are "Seeds of Life". There is nothing called a "Seed of Knowledge" in the NGE mythos.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 26, 2012 3:54 pm The SDSS... yeah, I got nothing. There's pretty much no reason for those to actually exist, especially since they contain instructions on dealing with a problem (2 seeds 1 planet ) that never should have happened in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:25 pm NemZ wrote: There's pretty much no reason for those to actually exist, especially since they contain instructions on dealing with a problem (2 seeds 1 planet ) that never should have happened in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Oct 26, 2012 4:49 pm Is it even possible for FAR to have "decedents?" As I've been saying, I feel like the Chamber of Guf, which is established as having a set number of souls, just contains the exact souls of the super-society that was the FAR. But then the question of "How did the original FAR, whoever it or they were, have decedents if there is a set number of souls?" comes up and spinning-backhands my notion of the relationship between Guf and FAR in the balls. Anyone have an answer or a bag of ice? EDIT: Also, to Bagheera - if the S/DSS doesn't specifically lay out what is to happen, how could Seele and Nerv know that there would be the creation and arrival of Tabris? How would the scrolls have known a Lilim and Adam were going to make contact to forge a new Angel?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 5:01 pm Merkaba wrote: Is it even possible for FAR to have "decedents?"

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Oct 26, 2012 5:12 pm So why is it made a point that the Chamber of Guf is finally empty during one of the episodes? Wouldn't that imply that the last of the original FAR souls finally took physical form? And, regarding Instrumentality, is Complementation only possible with every soul in existence? I ask because, if new souls are able to created, and say we compare two planets that have evolved slightly differently after the arrival of Lilim and one has far more souls than the other, how could they both lead to perfect Complementation (one having more parts than its original state)? (Relating to the question at the beginning of this paragraph, can Complementation occur with the absence of original parts or addition of new ones?) I really feel that it would have to be a set number of souls, which is the only way to put all of the fractions of the "many peoples together as one" back into place perfectly. I don't see how new souls can be created if souls are eternal (as evidenced by people who died prior to 3I being around in Instrumentality). If they have no end, why would they have a beginning? I wasn't a math major in college, but it seems that infinity/eternity goes both ways through time. I hope all this dribble makes sense. EDITED to make slightly more sensible. EDITED AGAIN to add this thought I had while PMing with Lurkis: Doesn't it seem odd that Ritsuko mentions the Chamber of Guf being empty just a short time before Instrumentality happens? Seele was following a certain schedule... Is it coincidental that the timeframe of Guf becoming empty (which took billions of years) and Instrumentality needing to occur lined up just a short time apart? Too coincidental.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Oct 26, 2012 5:25 pm Merkaba: This is becoming somewhat tangential from Dead Sea Scrolls, but I'll address the points in brief. Ritsuko mentions "The Chamber of Guf was empty" during her rant at the end of episode 23. In-context, she is talking about the creation of the Evas and Rei, and why the Evas weren't born with souls but Rei was. There are some good threads in the archives offering suggestions on interpreting the dialogue here. I could try tracking them down if you're interested. I doubt complementation requires "every soul in existence", considering the original FAR souls are strewn across the Milky Way. Instrumentality is also probably a more dynamic process than that you're making it sound, but that discussion would be better suited to a separate thread. Souls probably have a "lifespan" in NGE, but that too is a discussion for another thread. We've had some very detailed threads about soul metaphysics, if you're interested.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 29, 2012 2:01 pm Xard wrote: it's....better not to think to deeply about DSS as they're just about the most bizarre and mysterious part of the whole series mythology.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Oct 29, 2012 2:09 pm Reichu wrote: Merkaba: This is becoming somewhat tangential from Dead Sea Scrolls, but I'll address the points in brief. Ritsuko mentions "The Chamber of Guf was empty" during her rant at the end of episode 23. In-context, she is talking about the creation of the Evas and Rei, and why the Evas weren't born with souls but Rei was. There are some good threads in the archives offering suggestions on interpreting the dialogue here. I could try tracking them down if you're interested. I doubt complementation requires "every soul in existence", considering the original FAR souls are strewn across the Milky Way. Instrumentality is also probably a more dynamic process than that you're making it sound, but that discussion would be better suited to a separate thread. Souls probably have a "lifespan" in NGE, but that too is a discussion for another thread. We've had some very detailed threads about soul metaphysics, if you're interested.

Instrumentality is also probably a more dynamic process than that you're making it sound, but that discussion would be better suited to a separate thread.
